candidate_problems.text: What concrete institutional arrangements can generate genuinely independent regulatory knowledge and operational capacity in complex, fast-moving domains without simply recreating dependence through contractors, secondments, vendor tools, or industry-trained personnel?

What are the boundary conditions and counterexamples to the conjecture: in which domains does sustained contact not lead to structural capture, and what load-bearing features (e.g. observability, standardization, public data, slow change, adversarial redundancy, independent testing infrastructure) explain the difference?

How should structural capture be detected and measured empirically, distinguishing epistemic dependence from ordinary consultation or expertise-sharing, so that the theory yields testable indicators rather than a near-tautological claim that all informed regulation is captured?
